screenshot_note: IMPORTANT: The live URL https://osortega.github.io/bloberto-office/ serves 'Bloberto's Office' — a completely different project by the same author — not the Emergent World simulation. Screenshots (qa110-desktop.png / qa110-mobile.png) therefore show the wrong app. All code analysis below is based on the local /workspace/src/ source files for the Emergent World simulation.

suggestions.title: Stop blowing out the DOM on every tick
suggestions.description: renderAll() is called on every tick event, and inside it every major panel (regions, log, inspector) does a full innerHTML replacement. At 10x speed that is 10 full DOM rebuilds per second. renderLog() alone creates and destroys 200 DOM nodes per tick. The fix is incremental updates: prepend new log entries instead of rebuilding the list, and do targeted attribute/width updates on existing resource bar fills rather than recreating all region cards. This is the single biggest source of jank and wasted CPU.

suggestions.title: Event log is unusable at any speed above 1x
suggestions.description: The log is rebuilt newest-first every tick. At 2x+ speed entries flash past before they can be read. There is also no filtering — idle and rest events (which fire for every citizen every tick) completely bury deaths, combats, and trades. Add (1) type-filter toggle chips above the log (critical/social/resource/all), and (2) pause-on-event toggle so the simulation auto-stops when a death or extinction fires. The underlying data model supports all of this — it's purely a UI gap.

suggestions.title: Citizen links are inaccessible spans — break keyboard nav entirely
suggestions.description: Every .clink element is a <span data-id='...'> with a click handler. Spans are not keyboard-focusable and invisible to screen readers. Replace them with <button type='button' class='clink' data-id='...'> elements (button reset styles can handle the visual) or add tabindex='0' plus keydown handlers for Enter/Space. The population bar avatars have the same problem. This is a WCAG 2.1 SC 2.1.1 failure.

suggestions.title: Event log has no ARIA live region — screen readers see nothing
suggestions.description: The event log is the heart of the simulation but is completely invisible to assistive technology. Add role='log' aria-live='polite' aria-atomic='false' to #el-log so screen readers announce new entries. The panel header should get role='heading' aria-level='2'. The inspector panel needs aria-live='polite' so updates to the selected citizen are announced. Without these, the entire experience is inaccessible.

suggestions.title: Dead citizens accumulate in _citizens array forever
suggestions.description: Simulation._citizens is never pruned. Dead citizens remain in the array and are serialized via toJSON() on every single tick (getState() maps over all of them). In a long run with many deaths, this means an ever-growing array of dead citizen objects, each with memory[], relationships{}, and inventory{} maps being cloned every tick. Either move dead citizens to a separate _deadCitizens archive after death (still accessible for inspector lookups) or limit the total dead roster to the last 50.

suggestions.title: No single-tick step control
suggestions.description: The only temporal controls are Play/Pause and a speed slider. There is no 'Step →' button to advance exactly one tick while paused. This makes it impossible to study a specific situation — you either run at minimum speed (0.5x = 2 seconds per event) or miss things entirely. sim.runTick() is already exposed on the Simulation class; wiring a button to it is a one-liner.

suggestions.title: Relationship network is invisible — a hidden gem with no UI
suggestions.description: Every citizen tracks trust scores (-10 to +10) for every citizen they have interacted with, and these meaningfully affect trading, combat targeting, and talk decisions. But the inspector shows nothing about relationships. Add a Relationships section in the inspector listing the top 3 trusted and top 3 distrusted citizens as clickable links with a small score indicator. This would make the simulation's social layer legible and vastly more interesting to follow.

suggestions.title: Statistics panel — sim.getHistory() is completely unused
suggestions.description: The Simulation stores up to 500 tick results in _history, each containing full event lists, alive count, year, season. None of this data reaches the Dashboard tab (which appears to be unimplemented). A minimal chart showing population over time and a tally of deaths-by-cause would transform 'I wonder what happened' into 'I can see the winter of Year 3 killed four citizens.' Use a simple SVG polyline or canvas bar chart — no library needed.

suggestions.title: Extinction has no ceremony — the simulation just stops
suggestions.description: When all citizens die, stop() is called and an 'extinction' event is emitted, which adds one log entry. That is it. The simulation silently halts. This is the most dramatic possible event and deserves a full-screen overlay: cause of death breakdown, longest-surviving citizen, year reached, resource depletion timeline, and a 'New World' restart button. The history data to build this summary already exists.

suggestions.title: No UI to inject citizens mid-simulation
suggestions.description: sim.addCitizen(archetype, region) is a first-class public API, documented in the README, but has zero UI exposure. Adding a simple 'Spawn Citizen' popover (archetype select + region select + Spawn button) next to the Play/Pause button would let users intervene when a region is under-populated or an archetype is missing from the world. This is low implementation cost for high interactivity payoff.

suggestions.title: setSpeed() restarts the interval timer causing a visible tick skip
suggestions.description: setSpeed() calls stop() then start(), which clears and recreates the setInterval. If the user drags the speed slider quickly (likely — it's a range input), this fires many stop/start cycles, each potentially skipping or duplicating a tick. Debounce the slider input handler (e.g. 150ms) and reschedule the interval cleanly without full stop/start: clearInterval, recalculate interval ms, setInterval again.

suggestions.title: Health status in region pills uses color only — no text fallback
suggestions.description: Citizen pills in region cards show names with border/text color from healthColor() (green/yellow/orange/red). Color is the sole health indicator for living citizens in the regions panel. The inspector has explicit HP text, but the quick-glance region view does not. Add a tiny HP fraction (e.g. '8') or the health emoji (🟢🟡🟠🔴) as a suffix inside each pill. This also helps users with color vision deficiency.

suggestions.title: Skill progression is nearly invisible and almost never happens
suggestions.description: Skills can improve: crafting has a 10% chance on a successful craft, exploration has a 10% chance on an explore. Combat, farming, trading, and survival skills never improve at all. There is no event emitted when a skill increases, and no visual feedback. Consider: (1) emit a 'skill_up' event shown in the log, (2) add improvement chances for all skills based on activity, (3) show a brief highlight on the skill bar in the inspector when it changes. Skill growth is core to any simulation RPG.

suggestions.title: Boat and wall structures are crafted but have no gameplay effect
suggestions.description: CRAFTING_RECIPES defines 'boat' (8 wood, skill 4) and 'wall' (10 stone, skill 3) but the resolver and citizen decision engine have no logic for using them. A crafted boat goes into inventory as an item that does nothing. A wall similarly. Either remove them from the config until they are implemented, or implement basic effects: boats allow crossing to non-adjacent regions, walls reduce combat damage for sheltered defenders. Dangling no-op features erode trust.

suggestions.title: renderAll() causes double-renders for spawn/new_year/extinction events
suggestions.description: main.js registers handlers for 'spawn', 'new_year', and 'extinction' that each call renderAll(). These events are emitted from runTick() inside the auto-run loop, which also emits 'tick' — which also calls renderAll(). So each of these events causes two full renders per tick cycle. Remove the explicit renderAll() calls from the spawn/new_year/extinction handlers since the 'tick' handler will handle rendering. Extinction is a special case: stop() is called before 'tick' emits, so keep renderAll() there.

suggestions.title: Array.findLast() used in citizen.js — not available in all browsers
suggestions.description: citizen._urgentFood() calls this.memory.findLast?.(m => ...). findLast was added to V8/SpiderMonkey in 2022 but is not available in Safari before 15.4 or any version of Edge before 108. Since the project has no build step or polyfill, this silently fails (optional chaining returns undefined) on older browsers. Replace with this.memory.slice().reverse().find(...) or [...this.memory].reverse().find(...) for guaranteed compatibility.

suggestions.title: Weather effects are not explained to the user anywhere
suggestions.description: Region cards show a weather icon and label (e.g. '⛈️ storm') but give no indication of what storm means mechanically. Experienced users won't know that storm blocks movement and reduces combat rolls, or that rain gives a 1.2x harvest bonus. Add a tooltip (title attribute or CSS tooltip) to the weather display: 'Storm: harvest ×0.5 | movement blocked | combat penalty'. The data is already in WEATHER_EFFECTS in config.js.

suggestions.title: No visual fanfare for new year or milestone events
suggestions.description: Year transitions (new_year events with 🎆) and notable milestones (first shelter built, first trade, first combat death) deserve a brief visual celebration — even just a 1-second flash highlight on the topbar clock or a distinct sticky entry at the top of the log. Currently a new year is one line in the log that scrolls away instantly. These moments anchor the simulation's sense of progression and should feel significant.

suggestions.title: Memory system hints at depth that never surfaces
suggestions.description: Citizens record memories like 'harvested_grain @ river_valley' and 'moved @ mountain_pass', and the inspector shows the last 10. But the memory system is almost entirely unused by the decision engine — _urgentFood() has one findLast() call looking for 'food_seen' events that are never actually written into memory. The 'food_seen' memory event is never emitted anywhere in the codebase. Either wire up memory-guided decisions (citizens migrate to regions they remember having resources) or remove the unused memory lookup to avoid confusion.

suggestions.title: No persistence — refreshing loses all state
suggestions.description: There is no save/load mechanism. A long simulation run (Year 10+, complex relationships, several deaths) is lost on page refresh. Since sim.getState() already serializes everything to a clean JSON object, localStorage autosave every 10 ticks would be trivial. Add an 'Export' button and a 'Load' file input for sharing runs. This would dramatically increase replay value for users who get attached to their citizens.
